proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=96, 94.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +32.0pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8815 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
R-multiple 0.61 reflects D-8 Day-6 forced-exit dominance; if cohort R drops further on next archive, demotion auto-fires per §3
Portfolio is now 100% Choppy-concentrated; if regime rotates to Bear, this cohort will activate into a freshly-rotated book and concentration dynamics should be re-evaluated at that point
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=96, wr=94.7%, edge +32.0pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=96, Wilson lower 0.8815, +32.0pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (stable, 7 days) with zero Bear exposure in the 45 open positions, so activation is deferred and the existing 100% Choppy concentration is not compounded. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-05-10) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: UP_TRI × Metal: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, sector=Metal (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +25.5pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.72 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
R-multiple 0.72 reflects Day-6 forced-exit dominance (project_anchor §7 D-8) — true edge may compress if exit policy changes
Current UP_TRI open exposure is only 1 position; sector=Metal Tier-B boost may not generate near-term activations until UP_TRI signal frequency recovers
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +25.5pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification is robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +25.5pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.72. The sector=Metal filter narrows rather than amplifies UP_TRI exposure (only 1 of 45 open) in a Choppy-concentrated portfolio, and TAKE_SMALL sizing limits incremental concentration. Consistent with multiple prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-05-10) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_regime AND by_signal_type = Choppy regime (100%) + DOWN_TRI signal (88.9%) (100.0%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_regime AND by_signal_type = Choppy regime (100%) + DOWN_TRI signal (88.9%); recommended action: Pause new DOWN_TRI entries until signal share drops below 0.85; specifically block additions in FMCG, IT, and Auto sectors. Let natural Day-6 exits reduce concentration. If hedging is preferred, add a long index proxy (Bull regime hedge) sized to ~20% of the DOWN_TRI book to offset regime-flip tail risk.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A Choppy→Bull regime flip would unwind ~89% of the book (40 DOWN_TRI shorts) simultaneously with no statistical edge support — the Candidate-tier cohort (n=19) provides no validated probability that DOWN_TRI in Choppy is profitable, meaning the directional skew is pure exposure, not edge. Concentrated FMCG/IT/Auto short clusters amplify this by exposing the book to sector-specific reversals (e.g., FMCG defensive rally) on top of regime risk.

evidence: DOWN_TRI signal share = 40/45 = 0.889, exceeds 0.85 single-signal threshold
Top cell DOWN_TRI×Choppy×FMCG at 17.78% (n=8) approaches 20% cell threshold
Three concentration cells (FMCG, IT, Auto) all map to Candidate-tier cohort (n=19) — unvalidated edge
Combined FMCG+IT+Auto DOWN_TRI shorts = 18/45 = 40% of book in three sector clusters
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new DOWN_TRI entries until signal share drops below 0.85; specifically block additions in FMCG, IT, and Auto sectors. Let natural Day-6 exits reduce concentration. If hedging is preferred, add a long index proxy (Bull regime hedge) sized to ~20% of the DOWN_TRI book to offset regime-flip tail risk.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A Choppy→Bull regime flip would unwind ~89% of the book (40 DOWN_TRI shorts) simultaneously with no statistical edge support — the Candidate-tier cohort (n=19) provides no validated probability that DOWN_TRI in Choppy is profitable, meaning the directional skew is pure exposure, not edge. Concentrated FMCG/IT/Auto short clusters amplify this by exposing the book to sector-specific reversals (e.g., FMCG defensive rally) on top of regime risk.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Portfolio is 100% Choppy regime (above 0.80 threshold) AND DOWN_TRI dominates signal mix at 40/45 = 88.9% (above the 0.85 single-signal threshold). The DOWN_TRI×Choppy×FMCG cell at 17.78% just under 20%, but the broader picture is unvalidated: the matched Choppy×DOWN_TRI cohort sits only at Candidate tier (n=19), so the entire short-heavy book is unvalidated directional exposure that would unwind on a Choppy→Bull flip.
